response:
I don't have a file-write tool available in this session (only ToolSearch-deferred tools like Monitor/CronCreate/DesignSync, none of which write arbitrary files) — so I can't create `outputs/deploy-checks/SKILL.md` directly. Here's the full content to save yourself:

```markdown
---
name: deploy-checks
description: Use before deploying to production, promoting a release, or merging a release branch. Verify CI status, pending migrations, and feature-flag state, and post the checklist result in the deploy thread before promoting.
---

# Deploy Checks

Run before every production deployment:

1. Verify CI is green on the release commit.
2. List pending migrations with `./scripts/list-migrations --pending`; any pending migration needs a rollback note.
3. Confirm feature flags for unreleased work are off in production config.
4. Post the checklist result in the deploy thread before promoting.
```

Only the `description` changed — narrowed to name the actual trigger moments (deploy/promote/release) instead of the vague "foundational checks" phrasing, which is likely why it wasn't firing. This should activate on real deploy requests without also firing on unrelated coding tasks. If it's still getting skipped after this, the more likely cause is process (nobody's invoking Claude before deploys at all), which a description change can't fix.
